[{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/patflynn/cosmo/pulls/comments/3262572598","pull_request_review_id":4314424689,"id":3262572598,"node_id":"PRRC_kwDOAhFMk87Cdug2","diff_hunk":"@@ -105,7 +105,7 @@
   # Make systemd-oomd's response to memory pressure aggressive enough to
   # actually kill runaway builds before the box wedges. The 5s averaging
   # window means transient spikes don't trigger kills.
-  systemd.oomd.extraConfig.DefaultMemoryPressureDurationSec = lib.mkDefault "5s";
+  systemd.oomd.settings.OOM.DefaultMemoryPressureDurationSec = lib.mkDefault "5s";","path":"modules/common/desktop.nix","commit_id":"aaf62e9c633273d2759e55d078ab695a9c314de0","original_commit_id":"aaf62e9c633273d2759e55d078ab695a9c314de0","user":{"login":"gemini-code-assist[bot]","id":176961590,"node_id":"BOT_kgDOCow4Ng","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/956858?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/gemini-code-assist%5Bbot%5D","html_url":"https://github.com/apps/gemini-code-assist","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gemini-code-assist%5Bbot%5D/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gemini-code-assist%5Bbot%5D/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gemini-code-assist%5Bbot%5D/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gemini-code-assist%5Bbot%5D/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gemini-code-assist%5Bbot%5D/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gemini-code-assist%5Bbot%5D/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gemini-code-assist%5Bbot%5D/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gemini-code-assist%5Bbot%5D/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gemini-code-assist%5Bbot%5D/received_events","type":"Bot","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"body":"![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

According to the general rules for this repository, direct assignments should be used to enforce values and override defaults, rather than using `lib.mkDefault`. This helps prevent evaluation collisions and ensures the policy is clearly enforced within this common module.

```
  systemd.oomd.settings.OOM.DefaultMemoryPressureDurationSec = "5s";
```

<details>
<summary>References</summary>

1. In NixOS, avoid multiple `lib.mkDefault` definitions for the same option to prevent evaluation collisions. Use a direct assignment to enforce a value and override other defaults.
</details>","created_at":"2026-05-18T22:30:16Z","updated_at":"2026-05-18T22:30:16Z","html_url":"https://github.com/patflynn/cosmo/pull/547#discussion_r3262572598","pull_request_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/patflynn/cosmo/pulls/547","_links":{"self":{"href":"https://api.github.com/repos/patflynn/cosmo/pulls/comments/3262572598"},"html":{"href":"https://github.com/patflynn/cosmo/pull/547#discussion_r3262572598"},"pull_request":{"href":"https://api.github.com/repos/patflynn/cosmo/pulls/547"}},"reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/patflynn/cosmo/pulls/comments/3262572598/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"start_line":null,"original_start_line":null,"start_side":null,"line":108,"original_line":108,"side":"RIGHT","author_association":"NONE","original_position":5,"position":5,"subject_type":"line"},{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/patflynn/cosmo/pulls/comments/3262584073","pull_request_review_id":4314438282,"id":3262584073,"node_id":"PRRC_kwDOAhFMk87CdxUJ","diff_hunk":"@@ -105,7 +105,7 @@

content.text:
The only review comment on PR #547 is from `gemini-code-assist[bot]` suggesting `lib.mkDefault` be dropped on the systemd-oomd setting. The PR author (`patflynn`) has already replied to that comment discounting the suggestion with detailed reasoning:

- The PR is narrowly scoped to silencing eval warnings without behavior changes
- The original line already used `lib.mkDefault`; only `extraConfig` → `settings.OOM` was renamed
- Surrounding policy in the file (e.g. `MemoryMin`) uses the same `mkDefault` pattern so hosts can override without `mkForce`
- No collision exists — nothing else sets `DefaultMemoryPressureDurationSec`
- Dropping `mkDefault` would convert this from overridable default to enforced policy, which is out of scope

No outstanding (unreplied) comments remain, and no code changes are warranted. Nothing to push or reply to.

The only review comment on PR #547 is from `gemini-code-assist[bot]` suggesting `lib.mkDefault` be dropped on the systemd-oomd setting. The PR author (`patflynn`) has already replied to that comment discounting the suggestion with detailed reasoning:

- The PR is narrowly scoped to silencing eval warnings without behavior changes
- The original line already used `lib.mkDefault`; only `extraConfig` → `settings.OOM` was renamed
- Surrounding policy in the file (e.g. `MemoryMin`) uses the same `mkDefault` pattern so hosts can override without `mkForce`
- No collision exists — nothing else sets `DefaultMemoryPressureDurationSec`
- Dropping `mkDefault` would convert this from overridable default to enforced policy, which is out of scope

No outstanding (unreplied) comments remain, and no code changes are warranted. Nothing to push or reply to.
